purpose: Canonical governance rules for source-control mutation and merge legitimacy.
rules: No PREO -> no governed merge
Governance/runtime mutation -> requires SCO
PREO must bind exact PR head_sha
SCO must bind exact governed files
Merge proof must persist merge_commit_sha
Protected branch required for governed merges
Direct push to governed branches is a bypass path
No PREO_VALID -> no merge legitimacy
Required branch protection checks must resolve to emitted GitHub Actions job check-run names
Governance drift -> PREO invalid -> merge legitimacy NULL
Topology reconciliation signal SAFE_TO_MERGE is evidence only and grants no direct merge authority
Topology reconciliation signal TOPOLOGY_DRIFT -> merge legitimacy NULL
Topology reconciliation signal GOVERNANCE_DIVERGENCE -> merge legitimacy NULL
Topology reconciliation signal UNDECLARED_EXECUTION_SURFACE -> merge legitimacy NULL
Unresolved propagated drift -> merge legitimacy NULL
Topology impact divergence -> PREO invalid
Governance propagation mismatch -> reconciliation verdict invalid
Downstream legitimacy collapse -> governed merge denied
Active containment boundary -> merge legitimacy NULL
Recursive quarantine propagation -> PREO invalid
Federated containment divergence -> governance trust isolated
Downstream contamination spread -> merge authorization prohibited
Containment evidence may invalidate legitimacy but may NEVER authorize merge
Undeclared root authority -> merge legitimacy NULL
Sovereignty drift -> PREO invalid
Infrastructure mutation ambiguity -> containment required
Root authority topology divergence -> governance trust isolated
Root authority containment evidence may invalidate legitimacy but may NEVER authorize merge
Recursive governance semantic drift -> merge legitimacy NULL
Governance equivalence mismatch -> recursive containment required
Governance containment evidence may invalidate legitimacy but may NEVER authorize merge, proof, deployment, or execution
Runtime topology reconciliation ambiguity -> merge legitimacy NULL
Runtime topology equivalence drift -> merge legitimacy NULL
Runtime topology evidence may block merge or deployment but may NEVER authorize merge, execution, deployment, or legitimacy
Cross-registry reconciliation evidence may invalidate merge legitimacy but may NEVER authorize merge
Cross-registry proof lineage drift -> merge legitimacy NULL
Cross-registry replay drift -> deployment legitimacy NULL
Cross-registry ambiguity -> legitimacy NULL and reconciliation required
Cross-registry reconciliation may not authorize execution, proof, merge, deployment, authority creation, or automatic record repair
Direct push to governed branches -> merge legitimacy NULL and root authority evidence containment required
Force push to governed branches -> merge legitimacy NULL and root authority evidence containment required
Branch protection bypass -> root authority evidence only and may NEVER create merge legitimacy
Merge queue may only preserve legitimacy when required checks run against the enqueued exact head_sha and branch protection remains fail-closed
Squash merge may not reuse stale review or check evidence; PREO/SCO remain bound to exact PR head_sha and proof must bind resulting merge_commit_sha
Rebase merge may not reuse stale review or check evidence; PREO/SCO remain bound to exact PR head_sha and proof must bind resulting merge_commit_sha

merge_queue_policy.rationale: Merge queue may produce a combined commit SHA not covered by the original PREO/SCO candidate. Until merge-queue-specific PREO regeneration is implemented and required, merge queue is classified as MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL for governed branches.
merge_queue_policy.enforcement_mechanism: merge-governance-check workflow fails on merge_group trigger event
merge_queue_policy.closure_condition: Merge queue may be re-enabled only after a CI-003b implementation provides queue-SHA-bound PREO regeneration and that implementation is reviewed and merged.

unauthorized_mutation_path_closure.canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.
unauthorized_mutation_path_closure.closure_condition: No unauthorized reality mutation path exists.
